content.full_text:
Re
Farn Polrrrcer- PRACTTcES Coul,rrssroN
428 J Street e Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95914-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
September 1,2005
Irene S. Dick-Endri zzi, Treasurer
Ross Mirkarimi for Supervisor
721 Webster Street
San Francisco, CA 94117
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-05-177
Dear Ms. Dick-Endizzi:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the ..Act,,).1 please be aware that the
Commission does not advise with respect to past conduct. (Regulation 1g329(bXgXA).)
Therefore, nothing in this letter should be construed to evalu ati any conduct which may
have already taken place, and any conclusions contained herein apply only to prospective
actions.
QUESTION
May Supervisor Mirkarimi use his campaign committee's funds left over from his
iast election to pay for costs associated with hosting monthly art shows in his office at
city hall?
CONCLUSION
Supervisor Mirkarimi may use committee funds to host a monthly art show in his
office since the expenditures are reasonably related to a political, legislaiive, or
governmental purpose.
FACTS
Last year, Ross Mirkarimi successfully ran for district office on the Board of
Supervisors in San Francisco. His campaign committee has funds remaining from his
election.
' Got'"m*"nt Code sections 81000 * 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations,
4.Yi


File No. A-05-177
Page No. 2
Each month, Mr. Mirkarimi opens his office to the public to serve as an art
gallery. Local artists are chosen to convey the political beliefs of Supervisor Mirkarimi.
The costs involved in hosting these events include the price of food, drink, and
miscellaneous expenses.
ANALYSIS
Section 89512 controls the use of campaign funds associated with holding offrce.
Since Supervisor Mirkarimi is currently in office, this section is applicable to his use of
campaign funds. Section 89512 provides:
"An expenditure to seek office is within the lawful execution of the trust
imposed by Section 89510 if it is reasonably related to a political purpose.
An expenditure associated with holding office is within the lawful
execution of the trust imposed by Section 89510 if it is reasonably related
to a legislative or govemmental purpose. Expenditures which confer a
substantial personal benefit shall be directly related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose.,,
Assuming that Supervi*or Mirkarimi does not receive a substantial personal
benefit from the art showings,2 the expenditures need only be reasonably related to a
political, legislative, or govelrimental purpose. An art showing open to the public held in
his office at city hall appears to meet the definition of a political ir.po.". Tierefore,
committee funds may be used to pay the incidental costi associated with hosting such an
event.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General
By:
oen, PhD
Political Reform Consultant II
Technical Assistance Division
KSM:rd
I:\Adviceltrs\05- 1 77
2 See regulation 18960, copy enclosed.
